title: Xanthous Salty Slovakian Squid Gratin
description: Salty, dense, indulgent Slovakian dish featuring squid in a beurre-monte sauce, cooked in a low-glycemic banana leaf vessel.

ingredients.name: Heavy cream
ingredients.notes: low-fat option, e.g., almond milk or coconut cream

instructions: Rinse the squid tubes and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Cut the onion and garlic into small pieces.
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in a skillet over medium heat and sauté the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the squid tubes to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to firm up.
Sprinkle the flour over the squid and stir to combine.
Gradually add the dry white wine, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 1-2 minutes, until it thickens.
Stir in the heavy cream and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and add the mozzarella cheese, stirring until melted and smooth.
Season the squid mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Warm the banana leaves by wrapping them in a damp towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the gratin by spooning the squid mixture onto the center of each banana leaf, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Top each serving with a spoonful of beurre-monte sauce (see below).
Fold the banana leaves over the squid mixture to form a triangle and secure with a toothpick.
Place the gratin on a baking sheet and bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 12-15 minutes, until the squid is cooked through and the banana leaves are tender.
Serve immediately and garnish with chopped fresh parsley, if desired.
